labels.analysis: The MCP Server "Contabo (VPS) MCP Server" provides 124 tools focused on database operations tasks. Core capabilities include creation and generation, data access and reading, deletion and cleanup, modification and updates. Key tools: tool_search, retrieveTagList, createTag, retrieveTag, updateTag.
labels.reasoning: Primary label "Database Operations" assigned based on strong keyword matches in tool names and descriptions: record.
labels.custom_label: Data Access & Generation - Contabo (VPS) MCP Server

tools.name: tool_search
tools.title: Tool Search
tools.description: Search the available tool catalog to discover tools by name, description, or arguments. Use a regex pattern or natural language query to find relevant tools. Returns tool references that are automatically expanded into full tool definitions.

query.description: A regex pattern or natural-language query to search tool names, descriptions, and argument names. Maximum 200 characters.

mode.description: Search mode: "regex" for pattern matching, "bm25" for natural language. Defaults to "bm25".

tools.name: retrieveTagList
tools.title: List tags
tools.description: List tags - List and filter all tags in your account

tools.name: createTag
tools.title: Create a new tag
tools.description: Create a new tag - Create a new tag in your account with attribute name and optional attribute color.

name.description: The name of the tag. Tags may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per tag.

color.description: The color of the tag. Color can be specified using hexadecimal value. Default color is #0A78C3

description.description: The description of the Tag name.

tools.name: retrieveTag
tools.title: Get specific tag by id
tools.description: Get specific tag by id - Get attributes values to a specific tag on your account.

tools.name: updateTag
tools.title: Update specific tag by id
tools.description: Update specific tag by id - Update attributes to your tag. Attributes are optional. If not set, the attributes will retain their original values.

name.description: The name of the tag. Tags may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per tag.

color.description: The color of the tag. Color can be specified using hexadecimal value. Default color is #0A78C3

description.description: The description of the Tag name.

tools.name: deleteTag
tools.title: Delete existing tag by id
tools.description: Delete existing tag by id - Your tag can be deleted if it is not assigned to any resource on your account. Check tag assigments before deleting tag.

tools.title: List tag assignments
tools.description: List tag assignments - List and filter all existing assignments for a tag in your account

tools.name: retrieveAssignment
tools.title: Get specific assignment for the tag
tools.description: Get specific assignment for the tag - Get attributes for a specific tag assignment in your account. For this the resource type and resource id is required.

tools.name: createAssignment
tools.title: Create a new assignment for the tag
tools.description: Create a new assignment for the tag - Create a new tag assignment. This marks the specified resource with the specified tag for organizing purposes or to restrict access to that resource.

tools.name: deleteAssignment
tools.title: Delete existing tag assignment
tools.description: Delete existing tag assignment - Tag assignment will be removed from the specified resource. If this tag is being used for access restrictions the affected users will no longer be able to access that resource.

tools.description: List history about your assignments (audit) - List and filters the history about your assignments.

tools.name: retrieveSecretList
tools.title: List secrets
tools.description: List secrets - List and filter all secrets in your account.

tools.name: createSecret
tools.title: Create a new secret
tools.description: Create a new secret - Create a new secret in your account with attributes name, type and value. Attribute type can be password or ssh.

name.description: The name of the secret that will keep the password

value.description: The secret value that needs to be saved. In case of a password it must match a pattern with at least one upper and lower case character and either one number with two special characters `!@#$^&*?_~` or at least three numbers with one special character `!@#$^&*?_~`. This is expressed in the following regular expression: `^((?=.*?[A-Z]{1,})(?=.*?[a-z]{1,}))(((?=(?:[^d]*d){1})(?=([^^&*?_~]*[!@#$^&*?_~]){2,}))|((?=(?:[^d]*d){3})(?=.*?[!@#$^&*?_~]+))).{8,}$`

type.description: The type of the secret. Can be `password` or `ssh`

tools.name: retrieveSecret
tools.title: Get specific secret by id
tools.description: Get specific secret by id - Get attributes values for a specific secret on your account.

tools.name: updateSecret
tools.title: Update specific secret by id
tools.description: Update specific secret by id - Update attributes to your secret. Attributes are optional. If not set, the attributes will retain their original values. Only name and value can be updated.

name.description: The name of the secret to be saved

value.description: The value of the secret to be saved

tools.title: Delete existing secret by id
tools.description: Delete existing secret by id - You can remove a specific secret from your account.

description.description: The description of the Private Network. There is a limit of 255 characters per Private Network description.

tools.title: Update a Private Network by id
tools.description: Update a Private Network by id - Update a Private Network by id in your account.

name.description: The name of the Private Network. It may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per Private Network.

description.description: The description of the Private Network. There is a limit of 255 characters per Private Network.

tools.name: retrieveUserList
tools.title: List users
tools.description: List users - List and filter all your users.

tools.name: createUser
tools.title: Create a new user
tools.description: Create a new user - Create a new user with required attributes name, email, enabled, totp (=Two-factor authentication 2FA), admin (=access to all endpoints and resources), accessAllResources and roles. You can't specify any password / secrets for the user. For security reasons the user will have to specify secrets on his own.

firstName.description: The name of the user. Names may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user. Users may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per user.

email.description: The email of the user to which activation and forgot password links are being sent to. There is a limit of 255 characters per email.

enabled.description: If user is not enabled, he can't login and thus use services any longer.

totp.description: Enable or disable two-factor authentication (2FA) via time based OTP.

locale.description: The locale of the user. This can be `de-DE`, `de`, `en-US`, `en`, `es-ES`, `es`, `pt-BR`, `pt`.

roles.description: The roles as list of `roleId`s of the user.

tools.name: retrieveUser
tools.title: Get specific user by id
tools.description: Get specific user by id - Get attributes for a specific user.

tools.name: updateUser
tools.title: Update specific user by id
tools.description: Update specific user by id - Update attributes of a user. You may only specify the attributes you want to change. If an attribute is not set, it will retain its original value.

firstName.description: The name of the user. Names may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user. Users may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per user.

email.description: The email of the user to which activation and forgot password links are being sent to. There is a limit of 255 characters per email.

enabled.description: If user is not enabled, he can't login and thus use services any longer.

totp.description: Enable or disable two-factor authentication (2FA) via time based OTP.

locale.description: The locale of the user. This can be `de-DE`, `de`, `en-US`, `en`, `es-ES`, `es`, `pt-BR`, `pt`.

roles.description: The roles as list of `roleId`s of the user.

tools.name: deleteUser
tools.title: Delete existing user by id
tools.description: Delete existing user by id - By deleting a user he will not be able to access any endpoints or resources any longer. In order to temporarily disable a user please update its `enabled` attribute.

tools.name: resetPassword
tools.title: Send reset password email
tools.description: Send reset password email - Send reset password email for a specific user

tools.name: resendEmailVerification
tools.title: Resend email verification
tools.description: Resend email verification - Resend email verification for a specific user

tools.name: retrieveUserClient
tools.title: Get client
tools.description: Get client - Get idm client.

tools.name: generateClientSecret
tools.title: Generate new client secret
tools.description: Generate new client secret - Generate and get new client secret.

tools.name: retrieveUserIsPasswordSet
tools.title: Get user is password set status
tools.description: Get user is password set status - Get info about idm user if the password is set.

tools.name: retrieveRoleList
tools.title: List roles
tools.description: List roles - List and filter all your roles. A role allows you to specify permission to api endpoints and resources like compute.

tools.name: createRole
tools.title: Create a new role
tools.description: Create a new role - Create a new role. In order to get a list availbale api enpoints (apiName) and their actions please refer to the GET api-permissions endpoint. For specifying `resources` please enter tag ids. For those to take effect please assign them to a resource in the tag management api.

name.description: The name of the role. There is a limit of 255 characters per role.

admin.description: If user is admin he will have permissions to all API endpoints and resources. Enabling this will superseed all role definitions and `accessAllResources`.

accessAllResources.description: Allow access to all resources. This will superseed all assigned resources in a role.

apiName.description: The name of the role. There is a limit of 255 characters per role.

actions.description: Action allowed for the API endpoint. Basically `CREATE` corresponds to POST endpoints, `READ` to GET endpoints, `UPDATE` to PATCH / PUT endpoints and `DELETE` to DELETE endpoints.

resources.description: The IDs of tags. Only if those tags are assgined to a resource the user with that role will be able to access the resource.

tools.name: retrieveRole
tools.title: Get specific role by id
tools.description: Get specific role by id - Get attributes of specific role.

tools.name: updateRole
tools.title: Update specific role by id
tools.description: Update specific role by id - Update attributes to your role. Attributes are optional. If not set, the attributes will retain their original values.

name.description: The name of the role. There is a limit of 255 characters per role.

admin.description: If user is admin he will have permissions to all API endpoints and resources. Enabling this will superseed all role definitions and `accessAllResources`.

accessAllResources.description: Allow access to all resources. This will superseed all assigned resources in a role.

apiName.description: The name of the role. There is a limit of 255 characters per role.

actions.description: Action allowed for the API endpoint. Basically `CREATE` corresponds to POST endpoints, `READ` to GET endpoints, `UPDATE` to PATCH / PUT endpoints and `DELETE` to DELETE endpoints.

resources.description: The IDs of tags. Only if those tags are assgined to a resource the user with that role will be able to access the resource.

tools.name: deleteRole
tools.title: Delete existing role by id
tools.description: Delete existing role by id - You can't delete a role if it is still assigned to a user. In such cases please remove the role from the users.

tools.name: retrieveApiPermissionsList
tools.title: List of API permissions
tools.description: List of API permissions - List all available API permissions. This list serves as a reference for specifying roles. As endpoints differ in their possibilities not all actions are available for each endpoint.

tools.name: listObjectStorageCredentials
tools.title: Get list of S3 compatible object storage credentials for user.
tools.description: Get list of S3 compatible object storage credentials for user. - Get list of S3 compatible object storage credentials for accessing it via S3 compatible tools like `aws` cli.

tools.name: getObjectStorageCredentials
tools.title: Get S3 compatible object storage credentials.
tools.description: Get S3 compatible object storage credentials. - Get S3 compatible object storage credentials for accessing it via S3 compatible tools like `aws` cli.

tools.name: regenerateObjectStorageCredentials
tools.title: Regenerates secret key of specified user for the S3 compatible object storages.
tools.description: Regenerates secret key of specified user for the S3 compatible object storages. - Regenerates secret key of specified user for the a specific S3 compatible object storages.

tools.name: retrieveUserAuditsList
tools.title: List history about your users (audit)
tools.description: List history about your users (audit) - List and filter the history about your users.

tools.name: retrieveRoleAuditsList
tools.title: List history about your roles (audit)
tools.description: List history about your roles (audit) - List and filter the history about your roles.

tools.name: start
tools.title: Start a compute instance / resource identified by its id
tools.description: Start a compute instance / resource identified by its id - Starting a compute instance / resource is like powering on a real server. If the compute instance / resource is already started nothing will happen. You may check the current status anytime when getting information about a compute instance / resource.

tools.name: restart
tools.title: Restart a compute instance / resource identified by its id.
tools.description: Restart a compute instance / resource identified by its id. - To restart a compute instance that has been identified by its id, you should perform a restart action on it.

tools.name: stop
tools.title: Stop compute instance / resource by its id
tools.description: Stop compute instance / resource by its id - Stopping a compute instance / resource is like powering off a real server. So please be aware that data may be lost. Alternatively you may log in and shut your compute instance / resource gracefully via the operating system. If the compute instance / resource is already stopped nothing will happen. You may check the current status anytime when getting information about a compute instance / resource.

tools.name: shutdown
tools.title: Shutdown compute instance / resource by its id
tools.description: Shutdown compute instance / resource by its id - Shutdown an compute instance / resource. This is similar to pressing the power button on a physical machine. This will send an ACPI event for the guest OS, which should then proceed to a clean shutdown.

tools.name: rescue
tools.title: Rescue a compute instance / resource identified by its id
tools.description: Rescue a compute instance / resource identified by its id - You can reboot your instance in rescue mode to resolve system issues. Rescue system is Linux based and its booted instead of your regular operating system. The disk containing your operating sytstem, software and your data is already mounted for you to access and repair/modify files. After a reboot your compute instance will boot your operating system. Please note that this is for advanced users.

rootPassword.description: `secretId` of the password to login into rescue system for the `root` user.

sshKeys.description: Array of `secretId`s of public SSH keys for logging into rescue system as `root` user.

userData.description: [Cloud-Init](https://cloud-init.io/) Config in order to customize during start of compute instance.

tools.name: resetPasswordAction
tools.title: Reset password for a compute instance / resource referenced by an id
tools.description: Reset password for a compute instance / resource referenced by an id - Reset password for a compute instance / resource referenced by an id. This will reset the current password to the password that you provided in the body of this request.

sshKeys.description: Array of `secretId`s of public SSH keys for logging into as `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. Applies to Linux/BSD systems. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

rootPassword.description: `secretId` of the password for the `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. For Linux/BSD please use SSH, for Windows RDP. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

userData.description: [Cloud-Init](https://cloud-init.io/) Config in order to customize during start of compute instance.

imageId.description: ImageId to be used to setup the compute instance. Default is Ubuntu 22.04

region.description: Instance Region where the compute instance should be located. Default is EU

sshKeys.description: Array of `secretId`s of public SSH keys for logging into as `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. Applies to Linux/BSD systems. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

rootPassword.description: `secretId` of the password for the `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. For Linux/BSD please use SSH, for Windows RDP. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

userData.description: [Cloud-Init](https://cloud-init.io/) Config in order to customize during start of compute instance.

license.description: Additional licence in order to enhance your chosen product, mainly needed for software licenses on your product (not needed for windows).

period.description: Initial contract period in months. Available periods are: 1, 3, 6 and 12 months. Default to 1 month

displayName.description: The display name of the instance

defaultUser.description: Default user name created for login during (re-)installation with administrative privileges. Allowed values for Linux/BSD are `admin` (use sudo to apply administrative privileges like root) or `root`. Allowed values for Windows are `admin` (has administrative privileges like administrator) or `administrator`.

addOns.description: Set attributes in the addons object for the corresponding ones that need to be added to the instance

tools.name: retrieveInstance
tools.title: Get specific instance by id
tools.description: Get specific instance by id - Get attributes values to a specific instance on your account.

tools.name: patchInstance
tools.title: Update specific instance
tools.description: Update specific instance - Update specific instance by instanceId.

displayName.description: The display name of the instance

tools.name: reinstallInstance
tools.title: Reinstall specific instance
tools.description: Reinstall specific instance - You can reinstall a specific instance with a new image and optionally add ssh keys, a root password or cloud-init.

imageId.description: ImageId to be used to setup the compute instance.

sshKeys.description: Array of `secretId`s of public SSH keys for logging into as `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. Applies to Linux/BSD systems. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

rootPassword.description: `secretId` of the password for the `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. For Linux/BSD please use SSH, for Windows RDP. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

userData.description: [Cloud-Init](https://cloud-init.io/) Config in order to customize during start of compute instance.

defaultUser.description: Default user name created for login during (re-)installation with administrative privileges. Allowed values for Linux/BSD are `admin` (use sudo to apply administrative privileges like root) or `root`. Allowed values for Windows are `admin` (has administrative privileges like administrator) or `administrator`.

tools.name: cancelInstance
tools.title: Cancel specific instance by id
tools.description: Cancel specific instance by id - Your are free to cancel a previously created instance at any time.

tools.name: upgradeInstance
tools.title: Upgrading instance capabilities
tools.description: Upgrading instance capabilities - In order to enhance your instance with additional features you can purchase add-ons.   Currently only firewalling and private network addon is allowed.

privateNetworking.description: Set this attribute if you want to upgrade your instance with the Private Networking addon. Please provide an empty object for the time being as value. There will be more configuration possible in the future.

backup.description: Set this attribute if you want to upgrade your instance with the Automated Backup addon.   Please provide an empty object for the time being as value. There will be more configuration possible   in the future.

tools.name: retrieveInstancesActionsAuditsList
tools.title: List history about your actions (audit) triggered via the API
tools.description: List history about your actions (audit) triggered via the API - List and filters the history about your actions your triggered via the API.

tools.name: retrieveInstancesAuditsList
tools.title: List history about your instances (audit)
tools.description: List history about your instances (audit) - List and filters the history about your instances.

tools.name: retrieveImageList
tools.title: List available standard and custom images
tools.description: List available standard and custom images - List and filter all available standard images provided by [Contabo](https://contabo.com) and your uploaded custom images.

tools.name: createCustomImage
tools.title: Provide a custom image
tools.description: Provide a custom image - In order to provide a custom image please specify an URL from where the image can be directly downloaded. A custom image must be in either `.iso` or `.qcow2` format. Other formats will be rejected. Please note that downloading can take a while depending on network speed resp. bandwidth and size of image. You can check the status by retrieving information about the image via a GET request. Download will be rejected if you have exceeded your limits.

name.description: Image Name

url.description: URL from where the image has been downloaded / provided.

osType.description: Provided type of operating system (OS). Please specify `Windows` for MS Windows and `Linux` for other OS. Specifying wrong OS type may lead to disfunctional cloud instance.

version.description: Version number to distinguish the contents of an image. Could be the version of the operating system for example.

tools.name: retrieveImage
tools.title: Get details about a specific image by its id
tools.description: Get details about a specific image by its id - Get details about a specific image. This could be either a standard or custom image. In case of an custom image you can also check the download status

tools.name: updateImage
tools.title: Update custom image name by its id
tools.description: Update custom image name by its id - Update name of the custom image

name.description: Image Name

tools.name: deleteImage
tools.title: Delete an uploaded custom image by its id
tools.description: Delete an uploaded custom image by its id - Your are free to delete a previously uploaded custom images at any time

tools.name: retrieveCustomImagesStats
tools.title: List statistics regarding the customer's custom images
tools.description: List statistics regarding the customer's custom images - List statistics regarding the customer's custom images such as the number of custom images uploaded, used disk space, free available disk space and total available disk space

name.description: The name of the snapshot. It may contain letters, numbers, spaces, dashes. There is a limit of 30 characters per snapshot.

description.description: The description of the snapshot. There is a limit of 255 characters per snapshot.

tools.name: updateSnapshot

name.description: The name of the snapshot. Tags may contain only letters, numbers, spaces, dashes. There is a limit of 30 characters per snapshot.

description.description: The description of the snapshot. There is a limit of 255 characters per snapshot.

tools.name: deleteSnapshot
tools.title: Delete existing snapshot by id
tools.description: Delete existing snapshot by id - Delete existing instance snapshot by id

tools.name: rollbackSnapshot
tools.title: Revert the instance to a particular snapshot based on its identifier
tools.description: Revert the instance to a particular snapshot based on its identifier - Rollback the instance to a specific snapshot. In case the snapshot is not the latest one, it will automatically delete all the newer snapshots of the instance

displayName.description: Display name helps to differentiate between object storages, especially if they are in the same region. If display name is not provided, it will be generated. Display name can be changed any time.

totalPurchasedSpaceTB.description: New total object storage limit. If this number is larger than before you will also be billed for the added storage space. No downgrade possible.

tools.name: tool_search
tools.title: Tool Search
tools.description: Search the available tool catalog to discover tools by name, description, or arguments. Use a regex pattern or natural language query to find relevant tools. Returns tool references that are automatically expanded into full tool definitions.

query.description: A regex pattern or natural-language query to search tool names, descriptions, and argument names. Maximum 200 characters.

mode.description: Search mode: "regex" for pattern matching, "bm25" for natural language. Defaults to "bm25".

tools.name: retrieveSecretList
tools.title: List secrets
tools.description: List secrets - List and filter all secrets in your account.

tools.name: createSecret
tools.title: Create a new secret
tools.description: Create a new secret - Create a new secret in your account with attributes name, type and value. Attribute type can be password or ssh.

name.description: The name of the secret that will keep the password

value.description: The secret value that needs to be saved. In case of a password it must match a pattern with at least one upper and lower case character and either one number with two special characters `!@#$^&*?_~` or at least three numbers with one special character `!@#$^&*?_~`. This is expressed in the following regular expression: `^((?=.*?[A-Z]{1,})(?=.*?[a-z]{1,}))(((?=(?:[^d]*d){1})(?=([^^&*?_~]*[!@#$^&*?_~]){2,}))|((?=(?:[^d]*d){3})(?=.*?[!@#$^&*?_~]+))).{8,}$`

type.description: The type of the secret. Can be `password` or `ssh`

tools.title: Get specific secret by id
tools.description: Get specific secret by id - Get attributes values for a specific secret on your account.

tools.name: updateSecret

name.description: The name of the secret to be saved

value.description: The value of the secret to be saved

tools.name: retrieveUserList
tools.title: List users
tools.description: List users - List and filter all your users.

tools.name: createUser
tools.title: Create a new user
tools.description: Create a new user - Create a new user with required attributes name, email, enabled, totp (=Two-factor authentication 2FA), admin (=access to all endpoints and resources), accessAllResources and roles. You can't specify any password / secrets for the user. For security reasons the user will have to specify secrets on his own.

firstName.description: The name of the user. Names may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user. Users may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per user.

email.description: The email of the user to which activation and forgot password links are being sent to. There is a limit of 255 characters per email.

enabled.description: If user is not enabled, he can't login and thus use services any longer.

totp.description: Enable or disable two-factor authentication (2FA) via time based OTP.

locale.description: The locale of the user. This can be `de-DE`, `de`, `en-US`, `en`, `es-ES`, `es`, `pt-BR`, `pt`.

roles.description: The roles as list of `roleId`s of the user.

tools.name: retrieveUser
tools.title: Get specific user by id
tools.description: Get specific user by id - Get attributes for a specific user.

tools.name: updateUser
tools.title: Update specific user by id
tools.description: Update specific user by id - Update attributes of a user. You may only specify the attributes you want to change. If an attribute is not set, it will retain its original value.

firstName.description: The name of the user. Names may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per user.

lastName.description: The last name of the user. Users may contain letters, numbers, colons, dashes, and underscores. There is a limit of 255 characters per user.

email.description: The email of the user to which activation and forgot password links are being sent to. There is a limit of 255 characters per email.

enabled.description: If user is not enabled, he can't login and thus use services any longer.

totp.description: Enable or disable two-factor authentication (2FA) via time based OTP.

locale.description: The locale of the user. This can be `de-DE`, `de`, `en-US`, `en`, `es-ES`, `es`, `pt-BR`, `pt`.

roles.description: The roles as list of `roleId`s of the user.

tools.title: Delete existing user by id
tools.description: Delete existing user by id - By deleting a user he will not be able to access any endpoints or resources any longer. In order to temporarily disable a user please update its `enabled` attribute.

tools.name: retrieveRoleList
tools.title: List roles
tools.description: List roles - List and filter all your roles. A role allows you to specify permission to api endpoints and resources like compute.

tools.name: createRole
tools.title: Create a new role
tools.description: Create a new role - Create a new role. In order to get a list availbale api enpoints (apiName) and their actions please refer to the GET api-permissions endpoint. For specifying `resources` please enter tag ids. For those to take effect please assign them to a resource in the tag management api.

name.description: The name of the role. There is a limit of 255 characters per role.

admin.description: If user is admin he will have permissions to all API endpoints and resources. Enabling this will superseed all role definitions and `accessAllResources`.

accessAllResources.description: Allow access to all resources. This will superseed all assigned resources in a role.

apiName.description: The name of the role. There is a limit of 255 characters per role.

actions.description: Action allowed for the API endpoint. Basically `CREATE` corresponds to POST endpoints, `READ` to GET endpoints, `UPDATE` to PATCH / PUT endpoints and `DELETE` to DELETE endpoints.

resources.description: The IDs of tags. Only if those tags are assgined to a resource the user with that role will be able to access the resource.

tools.name: retrieveRole
tools.title: Get specific role by id
tools.description: Get specific role by id - Get attributes of specific role.

tools.name: updateRole
tools.title: Update specific role by id
tools.description: Update specific role by id - Update attributes to your role. Attributes are optional. If not set, the attributes will retain their original values.

name.description: The name of the role. There is a limit of 255 characters per role.

admin.description: If user is admin he will have permissions to all API endpoints and resources. Enabling this will superseed all role definitions and `accessAllResources`.

accessAllResources.description: Allow access to all resources. This will superseed all assigned resources in a role.

apiName.description: The name of the role. There is a limit of 255 characters per role.

actions.description: Action allowed for the API endpoint. Basically `CREATE` corresponds to POST endpoints, `READ` to GET endpoints, `UPDATE` to PATCH / PUT endpoints and `DELETE` to DELETE endpoints.

resources.description: The IDs of tags. Only if those tags are assgined to a resource the user with that role will be able to access the resource.

tools.title: Delete existing role by id
tools.description: Delete existing role by id - You can't delete a role if it is still assigned to a user. In such cases please remove the role from the users.

tools.description: List of API permissions - List all available API permissions. This list serves as a reference for specifying roles. As endpoints differ in their possibilities not all actions are available for each endpoint.

tools.name: start
tools.title: Start a compute instance / resource identified by its id
tools.description: Start a compute instance / resource identified by its id - Starting a compute instance / resource is like powering on a real server. If the compute instance / resource is already started nothing will happen. You may check the current status anytime when getting information about a compute instance / resource.

tools.name: restart
tools.title: Restart a compute instance / resource identified by its id.
tools.description: Restart a compute instance / resource identified by its id. - To restart a compute instance that has been identified by its id, you should perform a restart action on it.

tools.name: stop
tools.title: Stop compute instance / resource by its id
tools.description: Stop compute instance / resource by its id - Stopping a compute instance / resource is like powering off a real server. So please be aware that data may be lost. Alternatively you may log in and shut your compute instance / resource gracefully via the operating system. If the compute instance / resource is already stopped nothing will happen. You may check the current status anytime when getting information about a compute instance / resource.

tools.name: shutdown
tools.title: Shutdown compute instance / resource by its id
tools.description: Shutdown compute instance / resource by its id - Shutdown an compute instance / resource. This is similar to pressing the power button on a physical machine. This will send an ACPI event for the guest OS, which should then proceed to a clean shutdown.

tools.name: rescue
tools.title: Rescue a compute instance / resource identified by its id
tools.description: Rescue a compute instance / resource identified by its id - You can reboot your instance in rescue mode to resolve system issues. Rescue system is Linux based and its booted instead of your regular operating system. The disk containing your operating sytstem, software and your data is already mounted for you to access and repair/modify files. After a reboot your compute instance will boot your operating system. Please note that this is for advanced users.

rootPassword.description: `secretId` of the password to login into rescue system for the `root` user.

sshKeys.description: Array of `secretId`s of public SSH keys for logging into rescue system as `root` user.

userData.description: [Cloud-Init](https://cloud-init.io/) Config in order to customize during start of compute instance.

tools.name: resetPasswordAction
tools.title: Reset password for a compute instance / resource referenced by an id
tools.description: Reset password for a compute instance / resource referenced by an id - Reset password for a compute instance / resource referenced by an id. This will reset the current password to the password that you provided in the body of this request.

sshKeys.description: Array of `secretId`s of public SSH keys for logging into as `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. Applies to Linux/BSD systems. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

rootPassword.description: `secretId` of the password for the `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. For Linux/BSD please use SSH, for Windows RDP. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

userData.description: [Cloud-Init](https://cloud-init.io/) Config in order to customize during start of compute instance.

tools.name: retrieveInstancesList
tools.title: List instances
tools.description: List instances - List and filter all instances in your account

tools.name: createInstance
tools.title: Create a new instance
tools.description:
Create a new instance - Create a new instance for your account with the provided parameters.
ProductIdProductDisk Size
V91VPS 10 NVMe75 GB NVMe
V92VPS 10 SSD150 GB SSD
V93VPS 10 Storage300 GB SSD
V94VPS 20 NVMe100 GB NVMe
V95VPS 20 SSD200 GB SSD
V96VPS 20 Storage400 GB SSD
V97VPS 30 NVMe200 GB NVMe
V98VPS 30 SSD400 GB SSD
V99VPS 30 Storage1000 GB NVMe
V100VPS 40 NVMe250 GB NVMe
V101VPS 40 SSD500 GB SSD
V102VPS 40 Storage1200 GB NVMe
V103VPS 50 NVMe300 GB NVMe
V104VPS 50 SSD600 GB SSD
V105VPS 50 Storage1400 GB SSD
V106VPS 60 NVMe350 GB NVMe
V107VPS 60 SSD700 GB SSD
V8VDS S180 GB NVMe
V9VDS M240 GB NVMe
V10VDS L360 GB NVMe
V11VDS XL480 GB NVMe
V16VDS XXL720 GB NVMe

imageId.description: ImageId to be used to setup the compute instance. Default is Ubuntu 22.04

region.description: Instance Region where the compute instance should be located. Default is EU

sshKeys.description: Array of `secretId`s of public SSH keys for logging into as `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. Applies to Linux/BSD systems. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

rootPassword.description: `secretId` of the password for the `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. For Linux/BSD please use SSH, for Windows RDP. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

userData.description: [Cloud-Init](https://cloud-init.io/) Config in order to customize during start of compute instance.

license.description: Additional licence in order to enhance your chosen product, mainly needed for software licenses on your product (not needed for windows).

period.description: Initial contract period in months. Available periods are: 1, 3, 6 and 12 months. Default to 1 month

displayName.description: The display name of the instance

defaultUser.description: Default user name created for login during (re-)installation with administrative privileges. Allowed values for Linux/BSD are `admin` (use sudo to apply administrative privileges like root) or `root`. Allowed values for Windows are `admin` (has administrative privileges like administrator) or `administrator`.

addOns.description: Set attributes in the addons object for the corresponding ones that need to be added to the instance

displayName.description: The display name of the instance

tools.name: reinstallInstance
tools.title: Reinstall specific instance
tools.description: Reinstall specific instance - You can reinstall a specific instance with a new image and optionally add ssh keys, a root password or cloud-init.

imageId.description: ImageId to be used to setup the compute instance.

sshKeys.description: Array of `secretId`s of public SSH keys for logging into as `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. Applies to Linux/BSD systems. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

rootPassword.description: `secretId` of the password for the `defaultUser` with administrator/root privileges. For Linux/BSD please use SSH, for Windows RDP. Please refer to Secrets Management API.

userData.description: [Cloud-Init](https://cloud-init.io/) Config in order to customize during start of compute instance.

defaultUser.description: Default user name created for login during (re-)installation with administrative privileges. Allowed values for Linux/BSD are `admin` (use sudo to apply administrative privileges like root) or `root`. Allowed values for Windows are `admin` (has administrative privileges like administrator) or `administrator`.

tools.name: cancelInstance
tools.title: Cancel specific instance by id
tools.description: Cancel specific instance by id - Your are free to cancel a previously created instance at any time.

tools.name: upgradeInstance
tools.title: Upgrading instance capabilities
tools.description: Upgrading instance capabilities - In order to enhance your instance with additional features you can purchase add-ons.   Currently only firewalling and private network addon is allowed.

privateNetworking.description: Set this attribute if you want to upgrade your instance with the Private Networking addon. Please provide an empty object for the time being as value. There will be more configuration possible in the future.

backup.description: Set this attribute if you want to upgrade your instance with the Automated Backup addon.   Please provide an empty object for the time being as value. There will be more configuration possible   in the future.

tools.name: retrieveInstancesActionsAuditsList
tools.title: List history about your actions (audit) triggered via the API
tools.description: List history about your actions (audit) triggered via the API - List and filters the history about your actions your triggered via the API.

tools.name: retrieveInstancesAuditsList
tools.title: List history about your instances (audit)
tools.description: List history about your instances (audit) - List and filters the history about your instances.

tools.name: retrieveImageList
tools.title: List available standard and custom images
tools.description: List available standard and custom images - List and filter all available standard images provided by [Contabo](https://contabo.com) and your uploaded custom images.

tools.name: createCustomImage
tools.title: Provide a custom image
tools.description: Provide a custom image - In order to provide a custom image please specify an URL from where the image can be directly downloaded. A custom image must be in either `.iso` or `.qcow2` format. Other formats will be rejected. Please note that downloading can take a while depending on network speed resp. bandwidth and size of image. You can check the status by retrieving information about the image via a GET request. Download will be rejected if you have exceeded your limits.

name.description: Image Name

url.description: URL from where the image has been downloaded / provided.

osType.description: Provided type of operating system (OS). Please specify `Windows` for MS Windows and `Linux` for other OS. Specifying wrong OS type may lead to disfunctional cloud instance.

version.description: Version number to distinguish the contents of an image. Could be the version of the operating system for example.

tools.name: retrieveImage
tools.title: Get details about a specific image by its id
tools.description: Get details about a specific image by its id - Get details about a specific image. This could be either a standard or custom image. In case of an custom image you can also check the download status

tools.name: updateImage
tools.title: Update custom image name by its id
tools.description: Update custom image name by its id - Update name of the custom image

name.description: Image Name

tools.name: deleteImage
tools.title: Delete an uploaded custom image by its id
tools.description: Delete an uploaded custom image by its id - Your are free to delete a previously uploaded custom images at any time

tools.name: retrieveCustomImagesStats
tools.title: List statistics regarding the customer's custom images
tools.description: List statistics regarding the customer's custom images - List statistics regarding the customer's custom images such as the number of custom images uploaded, used disk space, free available disk space and total available disk space

tools.name: retrieveSnapshotList
tools.title: List snapshots
tools.description: List snapshots - List and filter all your snapshots for a specific instance

tools.name: createSnapshot
tools.title: Create a new instance snapshot
tools.description: Create a new instance snapshot - Create a new snapshot for instance, with name and description attributes

name.description: The name of the snapshot. It may contain letters, numbers, spaces, dashes. There is a limit of 30 characters per snapshot.

description.description: The description of the snapshot. There is a limit of 255 characters per snapshot.

tools.name: retrieveSnapshot
tools.title: Retrieve a specific snapshot by id
tools.description: Retrieve a specific snapshot by id - Get all attributes for a specific snapshot

tools.name: updateSnapshot
tools.title: Update specific snapshot by id
tools.description: Update specific snapshot by id - Update attributes of a snapshot. You may only specify the attributes you want to change. If an attribute is not set, it will retain its original value.

name.description: The name of the snapshot. Tags may contain only letters, numbers, spaces, dashes. There is a limit of 30 characters per snapshot.

description.description: The description of the snapshot. There is a limit of 255 characters per snapshot.

tools.name: deleteSnapshot
tools.title: Delete existing snapshot by id
tools.description: Delete existing snapshot by id - Delete existing instance snapshot by id

tools.name: rollbackSnapshot
tools.title: Revert the instance to a particular snapshot based on its identifier
tools.description: Revert the instance to a particular snapshot based on its identifier - Rollback the instance to a specific snapshot. In case the snapshot is not the latest one, it will automatically delete all the newer snapshots of the instance

tools.name: retrieveImageAuditsList1
tools.title: List history about your custom images (audit)
tools.description: List history about your custom images (audit) - List and filters the history about your custom images.

tools.title: List history about your snapshots (audit) triggered via the API
tools.description: List history about your snapshots (audit) triggered via the API - List and filters the history about your snapshots your triggered via the API.

tools.description: Cancel a specific domain - Cancel a specific domain

enum: Switch to a different Contabo product

reasonText.description: Reason Text when `Other` reason got selected while cancelling an domain

tools.title: Get auth code for a domain
tools.description: Get auth code for a domain - Get auth code for a domain by id

tools.name: retrieveObjectStorageList
tools.title: List all your object storages
tools.description: List all your object storages - List and filter all object storages in your account

tools.name: createObjectStorage
tools.title: Create a new object storage
tools.description: Create a new object storage - Create / purchase a new object storage in your account. Please note that you can only buy one object storage per location. You can actually increase the object storage space via `POST` to `/v1/object-storages/{objectStorageId}/resize`

region.description: Region where the object storage should be located. Default is EU. Available regions: EU, US-central, SIN

totalPurchasedSpaceTB.description: Amount of purchased / requested object storage in TB.

displayName.description: Display name helps to differentiate between object storages, especially if they are in the same region. If display name is not provided, it will be generated. Display name can be changed any time.

tools.name: retrieveDataCenterList
tools.title: List data centers
tools.description: List data centers - List all data centers and their corresponding regions.

tools.name: retrieveObjectStorage
tools.title: Get specific object storage by its id
tools.description: Get specific object storage by its id - Get data for a specific object storage on your account.

tools.name: updateObjectStorage
tools.title: Modifies the display name of object storage
tools.description: Modifies the display name of object storage - Modifies the display name of object storage. Display name must be unique.

displayName.description: Display name helps to differentiate between object storages, especially if they are in the same region.

tools.name: upgradeObjectStorage
tools.title: Upgrade object storage size resp. update autoscaling settings.
tools.description: Upgrade object storage size resp. update autoscaling settings. - Upgrade object storage size. You can also adjust the autoscaling settings for your object storage. Autoscaling allows you to automatically purchase storage capacity on a monthly basis up to the specified limit.

autoScaling.description: New monthly object storage size limit for autoscaling if enabled.

totalPurchasedSpaceTB.description: New total object storage limit. If this number is larger than before you will also be billed for the added storage space. No downgrade possible.

tools.name: retrieveObjectStoragesStats
tools.title: List usage statistics about the specified object storage
tools.description: List usage statistics about the specified object storage - List usage statistics about the specified object storage such as the number of objects uploaded / created, used object storage space. Please note that the usage statistics are updated regularly and are not live usage statistics.

tools.name: CancelObjectStorage
tools.title: Cancels the specified object storage at the next possible date
tools.description: Cancels the specified object storage at the next possible date - Cancels the specified object storage at the next possible date. Please be aware of your contract periods.

tools.name: retrieveObjectStorageAuditsList
tools.title: List history about your object storages (audit)
tools.description: List history about your object storages (audit) - List and filters the history about your object storages.
